timeline.note: 27% drop from canonical 161 baseline. Initial 'WTF' moment.

timeline.stage: ollama killed + cache nuked + DPM warmup
timeline.note: Eliminated environmental confounds. Regression remained — confirmed code-side.
Baseline restored. Regression isolated to merge content.
Confirmed via narrow revert. Cleanup-dead-wmma-kernels PR removed load-bearing kernels.
Restored other valuable master fixes (graph_launch, dispatch.rs, version bump). Perf still at canonical baseline.

+24% tok/s by collapsing 
{3,} to 

 at engine entry. The 200+ tok/s number user remembered came from this lever, which had been opt-in until now.

root_cause.primary: PR #32 cleanup/dead-wmma-kernels removed load-bearing kernels (gemm_hfq4g256_residual_wmma{,2,_k4}.hip) thinking dead. Dispatch table fell back to slower variants for some shapes, doubling per-cycle cost on 27B verify forward.
root_cause.secondary: HIPFIRE_NORMALIZE_PROMPT defaulted OFF until 2026-04-26 — undisclosed major perf lever. User's prior 200+ tok/s benches had it ON; recent benches without it appeared regressed even when not.
root_cause.smoking_gun_path_in_session: earlier in this session I deleted K4 references from dispatch.rs because the kernel file was missing — that file was missing because of PR #32. Should have flagged as a regression source then.
